original: Source: DOVA PHARMACEUTICALS INC., 10-Q, 11/8/2018 ARTICLE 1 DEFINITIONS 1.1 “Act” shall mean the Federal Food, Drug and Cosmetic Act, 21 U.S.C. § 301 et seq., as it may be amended from time to time, and the regulations promulgated thereunder.
ollama_generated:
Clause:
Revised for a slight change in meaning — the new definition expands the scope of the Act to include any subsequent amendments or regulations that may be enacted:

Article 1 DEFINITIONS
1.1 "Act" shall now encompass not only the Federal Food, Drug and Cosmetic Act, 21 U.S.C. § 301 et seq., as it currently stands, but also any amendments or regulations promulgated thereunder that may be issued in the future. This definition will remain applicable regardless of any alterations to the Act made over time.

original: 1.6 “Alternate Product ” shall mean a pharmaceutical product that is commercialized by Valeant or its Affiliates in the Territory and that is part of the Salix business segment of Valeant’s parent company, Bausch Health Companies, Inc. However, it's important to note that this term does not include the Product itself.

In this rewritten clause, I've shifted the responsibility from both parties to solely the party who is undertaking the activities and obligations as outlined in the agreement. This means that the Regulatory Requirements are now applicable only to the actions of that specific party, rather than to both parties as stated in the original clause.

original: 1.9 “Calendar Quarter ” shall mean each successive period of three (3) calendar months commencing on January 1, April 1, July 1 and October 1, except that the first Calendar Quarter of the Term shall commence on the Effective Date and end on the day immediately prior to the first to occur of January 1, April 1, July 1 or October 1 after the Effective Date, and the last Calendar Quarter shall end on the last day of the Term.
ollama_generated:
1.9 "Calendar Quarter" is defined as a continuous period of three (3) calendar months, starting on January 1, April 1, July 1, or October 1, depending on the commencement date. The first Calendar Quarter of the Term will begin on the Effective Date and conclude on whichever of January 1, April 1, July 1, or October 1 follows the Effective Date. Conversely, the final Calendar Quarter will extend until the Term's conclusion date.

This revision aims to clarify that each subsequent Calendar Quarter begins on the appropriate month, and both the first and last quarters may span beyond three full months due to the Term or Effective Date start and end times.

original: 1.15  “Compliance Report ” shall have the meaning set forth in Section 4.2.2(c).
ollama_generated:
1.15 The term "Non-compliance Update" shall be interchangeably used, as defined in Section 4.2.2(c).

This change weakens the obligation slightly by using a more passive term ("update") instead of the stronger and more active term ("report"). It also changes the meaning subtly to indicate that it's not necessarily a comprehensive document detailing all instances of non-compliance, but rather an update about such instances.

original: 1.16  “Confidential Information ” shall mean all secret, confidential, non-public or proprietary Know-How, whether provided in written, oral, graphic, video, computer or other form, provided by or on behalf of one Party to the other Party pursuant to this Agreement, including information relating to the disclosing Party’s existing or proposed research, development efforts, promotional efforts, regulatory matters, patent applications or business and any other materials that have not been made available by the disclosing Party to the general public. All such information related to this Agreement disclosed by or on behalf of a Party (or its Affiliate) to the other Party (or its Affiliate) pursuant to the Confidentiality Agreement shall be deemed to be such Party’s Confidential Information disclosed hereunder. For purposes of clarity, (i) Dova’s Confidential Information shall include all Product Materials unless and until made available by Dova to the general public (including through Valeant) and (ii) the terms of this Agreement shall be considered Confidential Information of both Parties.
ollama_generated:
1.16 "Confidential Data" encompasses all secret, confidential, non-public, or proprietary Knowledge, communicated in written, oral, graphic, video, computer, or other forms, shared between the Parties under this Agreement. This includes information pertaining to the disclosing Party's current or planned research, development, marketing, regulatory matters, patent applications, business strategies, and any other materials not publicly disclosed by the disclosing Party.

In addition, any information related to this Agreement shared between the Parties (or their Affiliates) pursuant to the Confidentiality Agreement shall be deemed to be each Party's Confidential Data disclosed under this contract. For the sake of clarity:

a) The Confidential Data of Dova will include all Product Materials, unless and until they are made available by Dova to the general public (including through Valeant).
b) Both Parties consider the terms of this Agreement as their own Confidential Data.

original: 1.41  “Non-Retail Institution ” shall mean any institution (other than the Specialty Pharmacies, Retail Pharmacies and Intermediaries) to which Dova (or its Affiliates or its Intermediaries) sells and/or ships units of Product during the Term, which shall include group purchasing organizations (GPOs), hospitals, clinics, long term care facilities and any outlets that are a member of an Integrated Delivery Network (IDN), and with which Dova or its Affiliates do not have data agreements which enables Dova to track shipments of Product from such institution to patients based on the Target Professional prescribing such Product.

original: Source: DOVA PHARMACEUTICALS INC., 10-Q, 11/8/2018 1.53  “Retail Net Sales ” shall mean, for each SKU of the Product:
ollama_generated:
Clause:
Adapted from DOVA PHARMACEUTICALS INC., 10-Q, 11/8/2018 (1.53) "Retail Net Sales" shall be defined, for each SKU of the Product, as the total sales revenue less any allowances, rebates, or discounts granted to retailers.

Changes made: The original clause defines Retail Net Sales as the gross amount received from each product's SKU. In the revised version, I have included allowances, rebates, and discounts granted to retailers in the calculation of net sales, thus weakening the obligation for the party involved to report higher revenue figures.

original: 1.54  “Sales Representative ” shall mean an individual employed and compensated by Valeant as a full-time employee as part of its sales forces and who engages in Detailing of the Designated Product (or the Alternate Product, as the case may be) in the Territory, and who is also trained with respect to the Product in accordance with this Agreement (including the Product Labeling and the use of the Promotional Materials) to deliver Details for the Product in the Field in the Territory.
ollama_generated: 1.54 The term "Designated Sales Agent" shall refer to an individual who is employed on a full-time basis by Valeant, remunerated as part of its sales force, and actively engages in Detailing for the Designated Product (or the Alternate Product, as applicable) within the defined Territory. This individual must undergo training regarding the Product, adhering to this Agreement, including Product Labeling and Promotional Materials usage, to deliver Details for the Product in the Field, within the specified Territory. However, it is worth noting that while Valeant will provide training, the ultimate responsibility for ensuring compliance with this Agreement lies with the Designated Sales Agent themselves.

original: 1.56  “Specialty ” shall mean (i) Target Professionals with a primary or secondary specialty designation of Gastroenterology, Colorectal Surgery or Proctology (excluding any such Target Professionals with a primary or secondary specialty designation of Hepatology (including Transplant Hepatology), in each case, as determined by data reported by Dova’s Third Party Data Source, subject to any adjustments determined pursuant to the process set out in Section 6.5, and (ii) all healthcare professionals with Nurse or Physician Assistant specialty designations affiliated with the Target Professionals described in subsection (i), as adjusted.
ollama_generated:
1.56 The term "Specialists" shall encompass (i) medical professionals primarily or secondarily specialized in Gastroenterology, Colorectal Surgery, or Proctology, except those with a primary or secondary specialty designation of Hepatology (including Transplant Hepatology), as identified by Dova's Third Party Data Source, subject to any revisions made according to the procedures outlined in Section 6.5. Furthermore, this definition shall extend to include all Nurse and Physician Assistant specialists associated with the aforementioned medical professionals, following any modifications.

This revision shifts the responsibility of identifying the excluded specialty (Hepatology) from the party receiving the services to Dova's Third Party Data Source. It also clarifies that the included healthcare professionals are associated with the specified medical professionals.

original: 3.2 Meetings and Minutes . Meetings of the JSC may be called by either Party on no less than thirty (30) days’ notice during the Term. Each Party shall make all proposals for agenda items and shall provide all appropriate information with respect to such proposed items at least ten (10) days in advance to the applicable meeting; provided that under exigent circumstances requiring input by the JSC, a Party may provide its agenda items to the other Party within a shorter period of time in advance of the meeting, or may propose that there not be a specific agenda for that particular meeting, so long as the other Party consents to such later addition of such agenda items or the absence of a specific agenda for such meeting, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld. The chairperson shall prepare and circulate for review and approval of the Parties minutes of each meeting within thirty (30) days after the meeting. Each Party shall bear its own costs for its members to attend such meetings.
ollama_generated:
Clause 3.2: Meetings and Minutes

The Joint Steering Committee (JSC) meetings may be initiated by either party with a notice period of at least thirty (30) days, during the contract duration. Proposed agenda items from either party must be submitted along with all necessary information to the other party at least ten (10) days prior to the meeting. However, under urgent circumstances necessitating immediate JSC input, one party may present its proposals within a shorter notice period before the meeting or request a meeting without an explicit agenda, given that the other party agrees to this expedited process or flexible agenda format. The consent of the other party for such exceptions shall not be arbitrarily withheld.

The chairperson is responsible for preparing and distributing draft minutes from each meeting for review and approval by both parties within thirty (30) days following the meeting. Each party will cover its own costs associated with attending these meetings.

original: 3.5 Marketing Sub-Committee .

3.5. ComMITtee for Marketing (hereinafter referred to as the "Marketing Committee")

The parties acknowledge that the responsibilities of the Marketing Committee are at their own discretion, and they shall not be held liable for any outcomes or consequences arising from their decisions, unless otherwise specified in this Agreement. Any actions taken by the Marketing Committee shall be considered as recommendations, not binding obligations, unless explicitly stated otherwise.

original: The Parties agree that such team may be added or removed by the mutual written agreement of the Parties without the need to amend this Agreement in accordance with Section 13.8.
modified:
The Parties have the discretion, at their sole and independent judgment, to unilaterally introduce or exclude team members, without requiring a formal amendment to this Agreement, as per Section 13.8, provided that such decision shall not be contingent upon mutual consent in writing.

This change shifts the responsibility for adding or removing team members from a mutual agreement to an individual party's discretion, while maintaining professional language and fluency.

original: (a) Commencing promptly upon completion of training of the Field Force Personnel that are engaged in Detailing the Product as described in Section 4.4.1 (but on the condition that Promotional Materials have been approved and delivered), Valeant shall deploy its Field Force Personnel that are engaged in Detailing to Detail the Product in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Subject to compliance with the terms of this Agreement, Valeant shall be responsible, in its discretion, acting reasonably, for determining the manner in which it allocates and prioritizes the Details, provided that, in so allocating the Details, Valeant shall take into consideration geographic territory, frequency of calls, prescribing levels and other reasonable considerations. Except as set forth in this Agreement, without the prior written consent of Dova (not to be unreasonably withheld, delayed or conditioned), Valeant shall not conduct any Valeant Activities, other than Detailing, with respect to the Product.
ollama_generated:
(a) Upon the completion of training for Field Force Personnel involved in Detailing the Product as outlined in Section 4.4.1, and upon receipt of approved and delivered Promotional Materials, Valeant will initiate deployment of its Field Force Personnel for Detailing the Product, in accordance with this Agreement's terms. Valeant shall retain the discretion to decide the allocation and prioritization of Details, acting reasonably and considering factors such as geographic territory, frequency of calls, prescribing levels, and other reasonable considerations. However, the decision-making power regarding the manner of detail allocation lies with Valeant.

Except for Detailing activities outlined in this Agreement, without Dova's prior written consent (which should not be unreasonably withheld, delayed or subjected to conditions), Valeant is prohibited from engaging in any other Valeant Activities related to the Product.

This revised clause maintains professionalism and fluency while subtly shifting responsibility for detail allocation decisions from both parties to Valeant and strengthening the obligation for Dova's consent for additional Valeant activities.

original: 4.2.2  Records and Reports.
ollama_generated:
4.2.2. Documentation Obligations:

In contrast to the original clause, this version places more emphasis on the recipient's rights regarding records and reports, making it slightly weaker for the obligor (the party required to perform the action). Here is a possible rewrite:

"4.2.2. Records and Reports. The Recipient shall have the right to access, review, and retain copies of all relevant documents and reports provided by the Obligor. However, the Obligor shall not be obligated to create any records or reports beyond those necessary for the fulfillment of its obligations under this Agreement."

This version of the clause still maintains professionalism while allowing the recipient more access to records and reports, but it also makes it clear that the obligor is not required to go above and beyond in creating additional records or reports.

original: (a) Valeant shall keep accurate and complete records, consistent with pharmaceutical industry standards, of each Detail and its obligations hereunder in connection therewith. Such records shall be kept for the longer of (i) [***] after the end of the Calendar Year to which they relate and (ii) such period of time as required by Applicable Laws. Within [***] following the end of each Calendar Quarter during the Term, Valeant shall provide Dova with a written report (each a “ Detail Report ”), setting out (i) the quarterly average number of Sales Representatives during such Calendar Quarter (calculated by taking the sum of the number of Sales Representatives employed by Valeant (or its affiliates) that have incentive compensation packages that comply with the terms of Section 4.1.3 on each Business Day of the Calendar Quarter divided by the number of Business Days in such Calendar Quarter) (the “ Quarterly Average Sales Force Size ”), and (ii) the aggregate actual number of Details for the Product made by its Sales Representatives during such Calendar Quarter, and the number of Details broken down by the name of the Target Professionals,. Through the JSC, the Parties shall agree on a mutually acceptable form of Detail Report.
ollama_generated:
(a) Dova shall have the right to request, upon reasonable notice, access to Valeant's records pertaining to each Detail and its obligations hereunder, as long as such records are maintained in accordance with pharmaceutical industry standards. These records shall be retained for a period of no less than the longer of (i) [***] after the end of the Calendar Year to which they relate or (ii) the minimum retention period mandated by Applicable Laws. Not more than [***] following the end of each Calendar Quarter during the Term, Valeant shall furnish Dova with a written report (each a "Detail Report"), detailing (i) the quarterly average number of Sales Representatives employed by Valeant (or its affiliates) with incentive compensation packages compliant with Section 4.1.3, and (ii) the aggregate number of Details for the Product conducted by these Sales Representatives during such Calendar Quarter, along with a breakdown by the name of the Target Professionals. The Parties shall mutually agree upon an acceptable format for Detail Reports through the JSC.

This revised clause places the burden on Dova to request access to records and shifts the responsibility for providing reports to Valeant while also allowing Dova more flexibility in terms of timing, as they can now request reports quarterly instead of being provided them automatically.

original: 4.3.7  Dova shall ensure that any patient assistance program used in connection with the Product (and the services performed thereby in connection with the Product) shall be operated in accordance with Applicable Law. original: 5.3.2  If not prohibited by any Government Authority or Applicable Law, Dova shall notify Valeant immediately upon receipt of any notice of inspection or investigation by any Governmental Authority related to or that Dova reasonably believes may impact the Valeant Activities. original: 6.3.2  Monthly Reports . Within fifteen (15) days of the end of each month within each Calendar Quarter, Dova shall provide to Valeant a written report setting forth Dova’s good faith estimate of the Net Sales and the estimated promotion fee payable in respect of such Net Sales for each of such calendar month and the Calendar Quarter-to-date period, together with its good faith estimates of each of the items described in Section 6.3.1 above (assuming there will be no adjustments made to the promotion fee pursuant to Section 6.1.2). original: 12.4  Effects of Termination . Upon the expiration or effective date of termination of this Agreement, (i) all rights and obligations of both Parties hereunder shall immediately terminate, subject to any survival as set forth in Sections 12.5 and 12.6, (ii) Valeant, at Dova’s direction,  shall immediately return to Dova or destroy in accordance with all Applicable Laws all Product Materials, reports and other tangible items provided by or on behalf of Dova to Valeant or otherwise developed or obtained by Valeant pursuant to the terms of this Agreement (other than Valeant Property) (and at the request of Dova, Valeant shall certify destruction of such materials if Valeant does not to return such materials to Dova), (iii) Valeant shall immediately cease all Valeant Activities with respect to the Product, and (iv) each of Dova and Valeant shall, at the other Party’s direction, either return to such other Party or destroy all Confidential Information of such other Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, each Party may retain archival copies of any Confidential Information to the extent required by law, regulation or professional standards or copies of Confidential Information created pursuant to the automatic backing-up of electronic files where the delivery or destruction of such files would cause undue hardship to the receiving Party, so long as any such archival or electronic file back-up copies are accessible only to its legal or IT personnel, provided that such Confidential Information will continue to be subject to the terms of this Agreement.
ollama_generated:
12.4 Consequences of Termination: Upon the termination or expiration of this Agreement, the following shall occur, unless otherwise specified in Sections 12.5 and 12.6:

(i) Both Parties shall be released from all rights and obligations under this Agreement, except for those that are intended to survive termination.
(ii) Dova shall have the option to direct Valeant to either return or destroy, in accordance with Applicable Laws, all Product Materials, reports, and other tangible items received or developed by Valeant in relation to this Agreement (excluding Valeant's proprietary property). At Dova's request, Valeant shall provide a certification of destruction for these materials if they are not returned.
(iii) Valeant shall cease all activities related to the Product immediately upon Dova's request.
(iv) Each Party may, at the other's direction, retain Confidential Information for archival purposes as required by law, regulation, or professional standards, or due to automated backup of electronic files causing undue hardship to the receiving Party. Such retained copies shall be accessible only to legal or IT personnel and will remain subject to the terms of this Agreement.

In this revised clause, the responsibility for returning or destroying Product Materials and Confidential Information has been shifted from Valeant to Dova, and the option to retain archival copies has been given to both parties in case of legal requirements or undue hardship. Additionally, the language has been adjusted for clarity and fluidity.

original: 12.6  Survival . Termination or expiration of this Agreement shall be without prejudice to any rights that shall have accrued to the benefit of any Party prior to such termination or expiration. Notwithstanding any expiration or termination of this Agreement, such expiration or termination shall not relieve any Party from obligations which are expressly or by implication intended to survive expiration or termination, including Sections 2.3, , 4.4.2, 5.7, 5.9, 6.3.6, 6.3.5, 11.1, 11.2, 11.3, 11.4, 12.4, 12.5 and 12.6, Articles 7, 8, 9 and 13 (to the extent applicable to implementation of the survival of the preceding Sections and Articles) and, solely as it relates to the last Calendar Quarter, Sections 6.1, 6.2 and 6.3, which shall survive and be in full force and effect.

original: Please see full Prescribing Information for DOPTELET (avatrombopag) www.doptelet.com About XIFAXAN XIFAXAN is a nonsystemic* antibiotic that slows the growth of bacteria in the gut that are believed to be linked to symptoms of overt hepatic encephalopathy (HE). It has been proven to reduce the risk of overt HE recurrence and HE-related hospitalizations in adults.
ollama_generated:
The comprehensive Prescribing Information for DOPTELET (avatrombopag) can be found at www.doptelet.com. Regarding XIFAXAN, it is an antibiotic that does not systemically impact the body, yet targets bacteria within the gut suspected of causing symptoms associated with overt hepatic encephalopathy (HE). Evidence demonstrates that it can lessen the likelihood of HE recurrence and hospitalizations related to HE in adult patients; however, its efficacy should not be presumed as a guarantee against such events.

*Non-systemic antibiotics do not enter the bloodstream or affect the entire body, but instead target specific areas like the gut.

original: *There is an increased systemic exposure in patients with severe (Child-Pugh Class C) hepatic impairment. Caution should be exercised when administering XIFAXAN to these patients.
ollama_generated: In instances of significant liver impairment, specifically Child-Pugh Class C, a heightened susceptibility exists for increased systemic exposure to XIFAXAN. It is strongly recommended that medical practitioners exercise due caution and carefully consider the potential risks associated with administering XIFAXAN to such patients.

original: •Talk to your healthcare provider before taking XIFAXAN if you have severe hepatic (liver) impairment, as this may cause increased effects of the medicine.
ollama_generated: Using XIFAXAN in instances of severe hepatic (liver) impairment is not advised without prior consultation with your healthcare provider, due to potential amplified impacts of the medication.

original: You are encouraged to report negative side effects of prescription drugs to the FDA. Visit www.fda.gov/medwatch or call 1-800- FDA-1088.
ollama_generated:
While it is highly recommended that you take the initiative to submit reports regarding adverse events associated with prescription drug use to the FDA, we understand that this may not always be feasible for every individual. As such, we would appreciate if you could make an effort to notify us about any negative side effects you may encounter, so that we can facilitate the process of reporting to the FDA by providing necessary support and resources.

The FDA can be reached through their MedWatch program at www.fda.gov/medwatch or via phone at 1-800-FDA-1088.
